When passing attrs dicts into widget methods across a loop, pass a copy per invocation (e.g., `{**final_attrs, 'id': ...}`) to avoid cross-iteration pollution from methods that mutate the dict.
When adding DISTINCT support to a Django aggregate subclass, set `allow_distinct = True` on the class; the base `Aggregate.__init__` and `as_sql` already handle the rest.
When adding a test for nested class serialization in Django's test_writer, define the nested class on the test class itself (not at module level) so __qualname__ produces the expected dotted path.
